source.title: Funding for farmers, growers and land managers
source.description: Apply for grants and other funding to increase productivity, manage your land to benefit the environment and support your agricultural business.

schemes.name: Sustainable Farming Incentive (SFI)
schemes.category: Manage your land to benefit the environment
schemes.notes: Window 1 opens from June 2026 for small farms and farms without an existing ELM revenue agreement. Window 2 opens from September 2026 for all farms.
schemes.description: SFI pays farmers and land managers to take up or maintain sustainable farming and land management practices that:

schemes.name: Countryside Stewardship Higher Tier (CSHT)
schemes.category: Manage your land to benefit the environment
schemes.grantValue: amounts vary depending on land management actions included in your CSHT agreement
schemes.description: CSHT pays farmers and land managers to manage land in a way that:

schemes.name: Landscape Recovery
schemes.category: Manage your land to benefit the environment
schemes.description: Landscape Recovery pays groups of farmers and land managers to do long-term, large-scale projects together. Land must be in England and consist of at least 500 connected hectares.

schemes.name: Capital items
schemes.category: Manage your land to benefit the environment
schemes.notes: Capital Grants will open in July 2026. Scheme guidance and eligibility will be published ahead of applications opening.
schemes.description: You can get paid for capital items through various capital grants to help you carry out management actions and improve environmental outcomes.

schemes.name: Farming Equipment and Technology Fund (FETF)
schemes.category: Invest in equipment, technology and infrastructure to increase productivity
schemes.grantValue: between £1,000 and £25,000 for each grant theme
schemes.description: The Farming Equipment and Technology Fund (FETF) has 3 grants to help you buy items to:

schemes.name: Water Management grant (round 2)
schemes.category: Invest in equipment, technology and infrastructure to increase productivity

schemes.name: Slurry Infrastructure grant (round 2)
schemes.category: Invest in equipment, technology and infrastructure to increase productivity

schemes.name: Adding Value grant
schemes.category: Invest in equipment, technology and infrastructure to increase productivity
schemes.description: The Adding Value grant is closed.

schemes.name: Improving Farm Productivity grant (round 2)
schemes.category: Invest in equipment, technology and infrastructure to increase productivity
schemes.notes: Grant value: between £25,000 and £500,000 for robotic or automatic equipment. between £15,000 and £100,000 for solar photovoltaic (PV) systems.

schemes.name: Farming in protected landscapes
schemes.openDate: apply at any time of year
schemes.grantValue: amounts vary depending on your project
schemes.description: Through the Farming in Protected Landscapes (FiPL) programme, you can apply for funding if you’re a farmer or land manager within an area of outstanding natural beauty or national park in England. You must be planning projects that:

schemes.name: Get funding to improve animal health and welfare
schemes.openDate: apply at any time of year
schemes.grantValue: from £215 to £923 per review or follow-up depending on livestock type
schemes.description: Farmers who keep cattle, sheep or pigs can get funding for a vet to visit their farm to carry out an annual health and welfare review, and an endemic disease follow-up.

schemes.name: Laying Hen Housing for Health and Welfare grant (round 1)
schemes.closingDate: invited applicants have until 11.59pm on 28 April 2026 to submit their full application
schemes.description: Egg production farmers can apply for a grant to add a veranda onto, or upgrade or replace, existing laying hen or pullet housing to improve animal health and welfare.

schemes.name: Calf Housing for Health and Welfare grant
schemes.description: Calf Housing for Health and Welfare grant

schemes.name: Creating woodland
schemes.category: Create or improve woodland and protect tree health
schemes.grantValue: up to £10,200 per hectare with an additional £12,700 per hectare if the woodland delivers wider benefits to society, nature recovery and the environment
schemes.description: The England Woodland Creation Grant (EWCO) provides funding to create new woodland on areas that are at least 1 hectare.

schemes.name: Creating regional woodland
schemes.category: Create or improve woodland and protect tree health
schemes.description: If your land is located within the catchment area of one of our local Woodland Creation Partners, you could apply for a regional woodland creation or tree planting grant that can be tailored to your circumstances.

schemes.name: Planning woodland
schemes.category: Create or improve woodland and protect tree health
schemes.description: The Woodland management plan offers grants for 10-year woodland management planning.

schemes.name: Protecting tree health
schemes.category: Create or improve woodland and protect tree health
schemes.grantValue: varies depending on the capital items chosen as part of your CS grant agreement
schemes.notes: Grant value: - up to 80% of felling costs per m3 - up to 40% of infrastructure costs
schemes.description: You can apply to the tree health pilot scheme to test different ways of slowing the spread of pests and diseases affecting trees in England. Grants are available for:
